sourceTitle: Bring live links into your Notion workspace with link previews

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion’s link preview feature?

options.text: To embed videos from YouTube directly into Notion pages
options.explanation: Link previews are for visualizing external tool data, not for embedding YouTube videos.

options.text: To display live, synced visualizations of external tools within Notion pages
options.explanation: Correct – the feature shows up‑to‑date content from tools like Jira, GitHub, Slack, etc., inside Notion.

options.text: To automatically generate AI‑written summaries of linked documents
options.explanation: Link previews do not create AI summaries; they show the actual content from the source URL.

options.text: To replace all existing Notion blocks with external app widgets
options.explanation: Link previews add blocks; they don’t replace existing Notion content.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an available paste option for a link in Notion?

options.text: Paste as preview
options.explanation: This is a supported option that creates a full preview block.

options.text: Paste as mention
options.explanation: This is a supported option that creates a condensed inline preview.

options.text: Paste as link
options.explanation: This is a supported option that inserts a regular hyperlink.

options.text: Paste as embed
options.explanation: The article does not list “Paste as embed” as a supported option.

questions.question: Who can authenticate an integration to create a link preview in Notion?

options.text: Only workspace admins
options.explanation: Admins are not required; any user can authenticate.

options.text: Only the person who originally connected the app
options.explanation: Each user can authenticate their own account with the integration.

options.text: Any user who has access to the Notion page
options.explanation: The article states that anyone with page access can authenticate to create a preview.

options.text: Only users with a paid Notion plan
options.explanation: The feature is available regardless of plan tier.

questions.question: When you paste a link as a “mention”, how does the resulting preview differ from a “preview” paste?

options.text: A mention creates a full‑width block, while a preview creates an inline snippet.
options.explanation: The opposite is true; mention is condensed and inline.

options.text: A mention generates a condensed, inline view of the content, whereas a preview inserts a separate block with a richer visualization.
options.explanation: Correct – mentions are compact and sit within text, previews are standalone blocks.

options.text: Both mention and preview produce identical blocks; the terms are interchangeable.
options.explanation: They are distinct options with different visual outcomes.

options.text: Mentions require authentication, while previews do not.
options.explanation: Both require authentication if it’s the first time the app is used.

questions.question: If a user pastes a link to an unsupported platform, what does Notion display?

options.text: A broken preview with an error message.
options.explanation: The article says unsupported links fall back to a regular link, not a broken preview.

options.text: A standard hyperlink without a preview block.
options.explanation: Correct – unsupported URLs are inserted as regular links with no preview.

options.text: A placeholder preview that updates once the platform becomes supported.
options.explanation: There is no placeholder functionality described.

options.text: Notion automatically removes the link from the page.
options.explanation: Notion does not delete the link; it simply shows it as a normal link.

questions.question: Which statement best describes how often link preview data is refreshed?

options.text: Link previews are refreshed automatically on a continuous basis, though exact frequency depends on the source app.
options.explanation: The article explicitly states continuous automatic updates with variable frequency.

options.text: They refresh only when the Notion page is manually reloaded.
options.explanation: Refreshes happen automatically, not just on manual reload.

options.text: Updates occur once every 24 hours for all linked apps.
options.explanation: Frequency is not fixed at 24 hours; it varies per source.

options.text: Data never updates after the initial preview is generated.
options.explanation: The feature is designed to stay up‑to‑date.

questions.question: Where can a Notion user manage the apps they have connected for link previews?

options.text: In the Settings menu → My connected apps section.
options.explanation: Correct – the article directs users to Settings > My connected apps to view and manage connections.

options.text: In the Workspace admin console under Integrations.
options.explanation: While admins have a console, the article specifies the Settings > My connected apps path.

options.text: On the Notion mobile app’s Home screen.
options.explanation: Management is done via the desktop/web Settings menu, not the mobile home screen.

options.text: Through the Notion API dashboard on developers.notion.com.
options.explanation: The API dashboard is for developers; regular users manage connections inside Notion settings.

questions.question: How does Notion handle multiple accounts for the same integrated app (e.g., Slack) when generating a link preview?

options.text: It always uses the first authenticated account and never prompts for another.
options.explanation: The article explains Notion will try the first account, then prompt if it fails.

options.text: It first attempts to unfurl the link with the initially connected account; if that fails, it prompts the user to authenticate the appropriate additional account.
options.explanation: Correct – Notion tries the existing account, then asks for the correct one if needed, and users can add multiple accounts in My connected apps.

options.text: It merges data from all connected accounts into a single preview.
options.explanation: Previews are generated from a single account that matches the link, not merged across accounts.

options.text: It blocks the preview entirely until the user disconnects all but one account.
options.explanation: Multiple accounts are supported; they do not block previews.

questions.question: An engineering team wants live Jira ticket and Slack message previews on a single Notion page. Which combination of actions ensures both previews update automatically?

options.text: Paste each link using “Paste as preview”, then authenticate the Jira and Slack apps once; the previews will stay synced.
options.explanation: Correct – using “Paste as preview” creates full preview blocks, and authenticating each app enables continuous updates.

options.text: Paste each link as a regular hyperlink; no authentication is needed because the links themselves will update.
options.explanation: Regular hyperlinks do not generate live previews and therefore won’t update automatically.

options.text: Paste as mention for both links and authenticate only the Slack app; Jira data will appear but won’t refresh.
options.explanation: Both apps need authentication; mentioning only provides a condensed view and still requires authentication for each source.

options.text: Paste as preview for the Slack link, but paste as mention for the Jira link, then authenticate only the Jira app.
options.explanation: Both links need their respective apps authenticated; mixing paste types isn’t required for updates.

questions.question: A user has connected two Slack workspaces to Notion. They paste a message link from the second workspace. What sequence does Notion follow to render the preview?

options.text: Notion first attempts to use the initially connected Slack account; when that fails, it prompts the user to authenticate the second workspace, then displays the preview.
options.explanation: Correct – the article outlines this fallback behavior when multiple accounts exist for the same app.

options.text: Notion reads the workspace ID from the URL and automatically selects the matching Slack account without any prompt.
options.explanation: The system does not automatically match; it tries the first account and then prompts if needed.

options.text: Notion shows an error stating that only one Slack workspace can be connected.
options.explanation: Multiple Slack accounts are supported; an error is not shown.

options.text: Notion creates a preview but requires a workspace admin to approve the second Slack connection before displaying content.
options.explanation: User-level authentication is sufficient; admin approval is not required.
